qa_pairs.question: What type of error does Valgrind report if you try to read or write to unallocated memory?
qa_pairs.answer: If you attempt to read or write to an unallocated pointer or outside of the allocated memory, Valgrind will report 'Invalid pointer access/out of bound errors'.

qa_pairs.question: What does a Valgrind error message for invalid pointer access or out-of-bounds errors typically look like?
qa_pairs.answer:
An error message for invalid pointer access or out-of-bounds errors from Valgrind will look like this:
`==9814== Invalid write of size 1
==9814==    at 0x804841E: main (example2.c:6)
==9814== Address 0x1BA3607A is 0 bytes after a block of size 10 alloc'd
==9814==    at 0x1B900DD0: malloc (vg_replace_malloc.c:131)
==9814==    by 0x804840F: main (example2.c:5)`

qa_pairs.question: What kind of error message will Valgrind produce if an uninitialized variable is used?
qa_pairs.answer: If you use an uninitialized variable, Valgrind will produce an error message such as 'Conditional jump or move depends on uninitialised value(s)'.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of Valgrind's error message for the usage of an uninitialized variable?
qa_pairs.answer:
Valgrind's error message for the usage of an uninitialized variable will look like this:
`==17943== Conditional jump or move depends on uninitialised value(s)
==17943==    at 0x804840A: main (example3.c:6)`
